threats.title: Lack of Data Classification and Access Controls
threats.description: Without knowing the data types being handled, it’s impossible to assess the potential impact of a breach.  Furthermore, the absence of robust access controls means sensitive data could be exposed to unauthorized users.  This could lead to data leaks, exposure of confidential information, and potential regulatory violations.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to data through vulnerabilities in the application or database, or through compromised user accounts.
mitigations: Implement a data classification scheme to identify and categorize sensitive data.
Enforce the principle of least privilege, granting users only the access they need to perform their jobs.
Regularly audit access controls and user permissions.

threats.title: Default Configurations and Unpatched Vulnerabilities
threats.description: The system is running with default configurations and lacks a comprehensive security program. This increases the risk of attackers exploiting known vulnerabilities or misconfigurations to gain elevated privileges, potentially leading to complete system compromise. The lack of regular patching further amplifies this risk.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of known vulnerabilities in software components, leveraging default credentials, or misconfigured permissions.
mitigations: Conduct regular vulnerability scans and penetration tests.
Implement a robust patch management process to promptly apply security updates.
Review and harden default configurations to remove unnecessary services and features.

threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks detailed information about its authentication mechanisms. This could lead to attackers bypassing authentication controls through brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication process itself.  Without knowing the authentication protocols used (e.g., basic authentication, OAuth), the potential for compromise is significant.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in the authentication service, brute-force attacks targeting weak passwords, or credential theft via phishing.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all user accounts.
Enforce strong password policies (minimum length, complexity, regular password changes).
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity and implement intrusion detection systems.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Requests
threats.description: The system's architecture is unknown, but without safeguards, it’s vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks. An attacker could flood the system with requests, overwhelming its resources and rendering it unavailable to legitimate users. The lack of rate limiting or traffic shaping mechanisms exacerbates this risk.
threats.affected_component: Web Servers, Application Servers, Database Servers
threats.attack_vector: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, overwhelming the system with a high volume of requests.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Utilize a Content Delivery Network (CDN) to distribute traffic and mitigate DDoS attacks.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.

threats.title: Unvalidated User Input
threats.description: The system’s design doesn’t explicitly state how user input is validated. This creates an opportunity for attackers to inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting) or manipulate data by submitting crafted input.  Without proper input sanitization and validation, the system is vulnerable to data corruption and potential compromise.
threats.attack_vector: Submission of malicious input through forms, URLs, or API requests designed to exploit vulnerabilities in the application’s code.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

priorities: High - Information Disclosure & Elevation of Privilege

metadata.error: ReportRepository.create() got an unexpected keyword argument 'format'

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api,database threat

rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Data Classification and Access Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Default Configurations and Unpatched Vulnerabilities
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Requests
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Unvalidated User Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
